ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term exists for salted egg quail egg onstik; mapped to broader egg food product class. Specific salted egg quail egg skewer term not present in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers cooked quail eggs broadly; no specific salted egg coating or skewer preparation code exists. Used as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific preparation. SNOMED CT does not enumerate individual egg dish preparations at this specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one skewer of approximately 3 to 4 salted egg coated quail eggs, weighing around 60 grams, commonly sold as a street snack or hawker side dish in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Salted Egg Quail Egg Onstik — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy 55 kcal, protein 10.7 g, fat 0.0 g, carbohydrate 2.36 g per 100 g) taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Salted Egg Quail Egg Onstik (cr_id G00010400). Secondary nutrients (sodium, saturated fat, dietary fibre, sugar) estimated from HPB compositional data for salted egg preparations and quail egg entries; see notes for detail.

gi_source.title: N/A — Negligible carbohydrate egg-based dish; GI not applicable
gi_source.source_match_notes: Salted Egg Quail Egg Onstik contains only 2.36 g carbohydrate per 100 g, which is negligible. Egg-based dishes with minimal carbohydrate content do not produce a meaningful glycemic response and GI testing is not performed for such foods. University of Sydney GI Database does not list a GI value for this food category. glycemic_index is set to the sentinel value 0 to satisfy numeric field requirements; gi_category is set to not_applicable to signal that GI measurement is not meaningful for this food. GL per serving is 0.0 based on negligible carbohydrate load (2.36 g/100 g × 60 g serving = 1.4 g carbohydrate per serving; GL effectively 0 given no measurable GI response).

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium from salted duck egg yolk coating (~700 mg/100 g), exceeding the 600 mg/100 g threshold for hypertension concern
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Salted Egg Quail Egg Onstik due to its very high sodium content (~700 mg/100 g from salted egg yolk), which significantly exceeds safe sodium thresholds and contributes to elevated blood pressure; deep-fried preparation further adds saturated fat that promotes arterial stiffness.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (2.36 g/100 g) and high protein (10.7 g/100 g) support glycemic control, but deep-fried preparation and high cholesterol from quail eggs and salted egg yolk require monitoring
type2_diabetes.guidance: Salted Egg Quail Egg Onstik has a very low carbohydrate content of 2.36 g/100 g and high protein at 10.7 g/100 g, which moderates post-meal glucose response; however, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit frequency due to high cholesterol (~350 mg/100 g), high sodium (~700 mg/100 g), and deep-fried preparation increasing cardiovascular risk, and should avoid pairing with sugary dipping sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High cholesterol (~350 mg/100 g from quail eggs and salted egg yolk), very high sodium (~700 mg/100 g), and deep-fried preparation generating saturated fat and AGEs collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid Salted Egg Quail Egg Onstik due to its combination of very high cholesterol (~350 mg/100 g), very high sodium (~700 mg/100 g), and deep-fried preparation that increases saturated fat intake and generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), all of which contribute to atherosclerosis, arterial stiffness, and elevated cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates AGEs that worsen joint inflammation; very high sodium from salted egg yolk worsens RA-related tissue swelling; high cholesterol adds to inflammatory burden
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Salted Egg Quail Egg Onstik as deep-fried preparation generates AGEs that exacerbate joint inflammation, very high sodium (~700 mg/100 g) worsens RA-related tissue swelling, and high cholesterol (~350 mg/100 g) from quail eggs and salted egg yolk adds to the overall inflammatory burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salted Egg Quail Egg Onstik is a popular Singaporean hawker and street snack consisting of small quail eggs coated in a rich salted duck egg yolk mixture and deep-fried, typically served on a skewer (onstik). The dish delivers a distinctive savoury, umami-rich flavour with a slightly sandy, grainy texture from the salted egg yolk coating and a firm, bouncy interior from the quail egg. The preparation involves hard-boiling quail eggs, coating them in a batter or paste made from salted duck egg yolk, and deep-frying until the coating is golden and fragrant. Per 100 g, the dish provides 55 kcal, 10.7 g protein, 0.0 g fat (confirmed per HPB source entry G00010400; fat_g is confirmed zero by formulation as reported by HPB, though deep-fried preparation context suggests some fat absorption may not be fully captured), and 2.36 g carbohydrate. Sodium content is very high at approximately 700 mg/100 g due to the salted egg yolk, making it unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Cholesterol is estimated at approximately 350 mg/100 g from the combination of quail eggs and salted duck egg yolk. The very low carbohydrate content means glycemic impact is negligible, but the high sodium and cholesterol load make this a high-concern food for hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. It is commonly consumed as a snack, side dish, or street food in Singapore, and is associated with lunch and dinner occasions.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010400, confidence 0.95 per source record). Sodium and cholesterol values are estimated from HPB compositional data for salted egg preparations and quail egg entries; these secondary nutrients are marked accordingly. The fat_g value of 0.0 is confirmed per HPB source entry and documented with justification.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database entry G00010400 for this specific product formulation; this is an unusual value for a deep-fried dish and may reflect the HPB entry capturing only the egg component macros without full fat absorption from frying — consumers should be aware actual fat intake may be higher depending on preparation. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as quail eggs and salted egg yolk contain no dietary fibre. saturated_fat_g is set to 0.0 consistent with the HPB fat_g=0.0 source value; actual saturated fat from deep-frying oil and egg yolk may be higher in practice. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not available in HPB Singapore Foodo database for this entry as of 2026. potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data not reported in HPB Singapore Foodo database for this entry; USDA FoodData Central does not have a direct equivalent entry for salted egg quail egg onstik as of 2026. sodium_mg (~700 mg/100 g) and cholesterol_mg (~350 mg/100 g) are estimated from HPB compositional data for salted egg yolk and quail egg components; these are not directly reported in the HPB entry and are flagged as estimates. Glycemic index field is set to numeric sentinel 0 with gi_category not_applicable; this food contains negligible carbohydrates (2.36 g/100 g) and GI testing is not applicable for egg-based dishes with minimal carbohydrate content — the value 0 is a required numeric placeholder and does not represent a measured GI of zero.
